title: Caleana hortiorum
taxonomy: Caleana hortiorum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Caleana and is a part of the Orchidaceae family. The genus Caleana is a group of small to medium-sized terrestrial orchids native to Australia. The species Caleana hortiorum is one of the species in the genus and is also native to Australia. Caleana hortiorum is classified as a rare orchid species due to its limited geographic range and population.

intro: Caleana hortiorum, commonly known as the Greenhood orchid, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid native to eastern Australia. The Greenhood orchid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Caleana hortiorum include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
